text:
David Speigel: There you are.
David Speigel: You're still on mute, or you're not on mute, but your speaker is not working.
Vrijen Attawar: No, no.
Vrijen Attawar: Yep.
Vrijen Attawar: Okay.
Vrijen Attawar: That's half the problem solving.
Vrijen Attawar: Hello?
David Speigel: Hello?
Vrijen Attawar: Okay, so.
Vrijen Attawar: Okay, it's coming out.
Vrijen Attawar: Sorry.
Vrijen Attawar: Could you try one last time?
David Speigel: Testing, testing.
David Speigel: One, two, three.
David Speigel: Check, check, check.
David Speigel: Receipt.
Vrijen Attawar: Yeah, we'll just stick to this.
Vrijen Attawar: I don't know why my.
Vrijen Attawar: My headphones aren't connecting, but this is good enough.
Vrijen Attawar: How are you, man?
David Speigel: Good.
David Speigel: How are you?
David Speigel: My notes.
David Speigel: Oh, I'm getting an echo.
Vrijen Attawar: Doing okay.
Vrijen Attawar: Doing okay.
Vrijen Attawar: Having a.
Vrijen Attawar: Having a pretty good start to the year overall.
Vrijen Attawar: How about yourself?
David Speigel: Hopefully I'm going to be more, like, motivated.
David Speigel: I don't know what the right word is.
David Speigel: Effective.
Vrijen Attawar: I mean, effective would be nice.
Vrijen Attawar: Motivated will be nice to feel like two separate but related problems.
David Speigel: Yeah, I don't know.
David Speigel: I'll be super transparent.
David Speigel: Last year, I probably worked about 10 hours a week and made, like, 50k for the whole year.
David Speigel: Is bad.
David Speigel: And part of it was just me not wanting to, like.
David Speigel: Like, not getting motivated to do anything.
David Speigel: Like, I have some Runway, and, like, I didn't find anything that I was passionate about.
David Speigel: And so now I'm trying to, like, reframe my goals for myself, to, like, just pick.
David Speigel: Pick goals, like, inspire my passion in different ways.
David Speigel: I don't know.
David Speigel: Challenge myself.
Vrijen Attawar: Yeah, I mean, it's.
Vrijen Attawar: It's hard, right?
Vrijen Attawar: It's hard when you don't have.
Vrijen Attawar: When you don't have something to really push that creativity and push that excitement and sort of.
Vrijen Attawar: I don't know.
Vrijen Attawar: Do you.
Vrijen Attawar: Would you say you're more extrinsically motivated or intrinsically?
Vrijen Attawar: Seems like extrinsically, right?
David Speigel: It's a little of both.
David Speigel: I was actually talking with Finn about this, and he was saying, like, are you posting to, like, inform.
David Speigel: Are you posting just to, like, create art?
David Speigel: I don't know.
David Speigel: I think a lot of times I'm just posting to, like, amuse myself.
David Speigel: Like, some of.
David Speigel: Some of my stuff that I do, like, I just.
David Speigel: It's kind of intrinsic.
David Speigel: Like, if other people notice and like it, great.
David Speigel: If not, Like, I just.
David Speigel: Yeah, but.
Vrijen Attawar: But in terms of.
Vrijen Attawar: And that's fair.
Vrijen Attawar: I think that accurately describes your overall motivation mechanism.
Vrijen Attawar: But when it comes to, like, work and when it comes to, like, the.
Vrijen Attawar: The grind, as it were, like, what seems to motivate you there, I guess, would be my question.
David Speigel: Yeah, probably more extrinsically.
Vrijen Attawar: Right.
Vrijen Attawar: Like, it's like, the reward of, like, working with, you know, solving a problem or getting paid to solve a problem more accurately.
Vrijen Attawar: Probably that plays a big role.
Vrijen Attawar: But what, what, what were you, what do you think you were waiting for all year?
Vrijen Attawar: Like, if you have to describe, like the thing that.
Vrijen Attawar: Yes.
Vrijen Attawar: If it had manifested itself, you would have been like, this is it and jump right in.
David Speigel: I think some of it was kind of like not early retirement, but like just taking a break from, from years of the grind of like working for other people and being forced to like, not only work long hours, but also do like a disproportionate share of parenting and like, personal stuff.
David Speigel: And so, like, I finally got into a place where like, a lot of things were handled without me having to do them.
David Speigel: And I was like, wow, I can actually like, breathe and it feels good, like exist and not have.
Vrijen Attawar: Yeah.
David Speigel: You know what I mean?
David Speigel: So I was like, so this is.
Vrijen Attawar: Two, two adult kids, right?
David Speigel: I have one adult kid, one senior.
David Speigel: So I have a freshman in college, a senior in high school.
David Speigel: She turns 18 in May in high school, and a 4 year old.
Vrijen Attawar: Wow.
Vrijen Attawar: Okay.
Vrijen Attawar: Okay.
Vrijen Attawar: So, yeah, I mean, the four year old, definitely a little bit of a curveball, but at least the first two, that's, that's a long stint of parenting.
Vrijen Attawar: Right.
Vrijen Attawar: So I totally get that.
Vrijen Attawar: That's an extended period of like.
Vrijen Attawar: Yeah.
Vrijen Attawar: Putting, you know, having basically another job on top of your job.
Vrijen Attawar: Right.
Vrijen Attawar: And that's clearing up a little bit.
Vrijen Attawar: Yeah.
Vrijen Attawar: I mean, it's interesting, man.
Vrijen Attawar: I think like, generally when folks are at this point, they say to themselves, like, hey, okay, at this point, like it.
Vrijen Attawar: Let me just treat it like it's a job and just find something that like, pays the bills.
Vrijen Attawar: Now whether a lot of those jobs are going to exist in the world of tomorrow is, is a question.
Vrijen Attawar: I think it's a real question and actually speaks to maybe why optimizing for passion and interest might be a good thing in a manner of speaking.
Vrijen Attawar: But I think it's all just a matter of like, how you deploy it.
David Speigel: Right.
David Speigel: Yeah.
David Speigel: Well, I brought it up a little bit because I was curious.
David Speigel: As we're starting this new year, I wanted to talk with you today about like three, three topics for you, but also kind of for me.
David Speigel: So I brought, brought that up, like, I wanted to understand what's going, like, what's going on with career span for you because we kind of stop talking about it.
David Speigel: And I wanted to maybe like close the chapter on that or understand.
Vrijen Attawar: Totally, totally.
Vrijen Attawar: Yeah, they're happy to give me an update.
David Speigel: Yeah, I wanted to talk more about the stuff that you're doing with Zoe and, and other stuff because you seem to be, like, really connected into the.
David Speigel: The scene in, in New York and tech startups and that kind of stuff, which I'm still obviously very passionate about.
David Speigel: And then I wanted to make sure we spent a little bit of time talking about GLPs, because, yes, I'm curious about it.
Vrijen Attawar: Totally, totally.
Vrijen Attawar: We can do all three.
Vrijen Attawar: What order would you like to go in?
David Speigel: How about just that order?
Vrijen Attawar: Yeah, sure.
Vrijen Attawar: So number one was career span.
Vrijen Attawar: So we're looking to sell the company.
Vrijen Attawar: We're not looking to raise more capital or put more money into the engine necessarily.
Vrijen Attawar: So we have enough money to sort of keep things running for about two months.
Vrijen Attawar: And so we're hoping to sell in that time.
Vrijen Attawar: I'm going to sell to a larger company and essentially serve as, you know, we're still sort of sounding out what is it that folks are most interested in.
Vrijen Attawar: I think if you were to reduce all of hiring into a couple of discrete challenges, I think people pretended like they had solved matching, and now that they can't solve matching because the emperor has no clothes, they're switching to verifying and vetting, which is to say is this person is John Smith, actually John Smith from Arkansas, like he claims or whatever.
Vrijen Attawar: And two, as I am evaluating John Smith or Jane Doe or whatever in the process, am I able to evaluate them on an ongoing basis correctly via whether through video interviews or through human interviews, but like AI augmented or, you know, AI augmented interviewers, stuff like that.
Vrijen Attawar: So I think there are a couple of discrete challenges.
Vrijen Attawar: I think we're the only ones that have really solved matching in the sense that we are positioned to solve it if we nail sourcing.
Vrijen Attawar: But what I'm seeing people want to buy is sourcing because they're jaded and they don't really believe that some magic matching tech will save the day.
Vrijen Attawar: The more concrete thing they're willing to invest in is do you have a pool of users that no one else has access to?
Vrijen Attawar: So that's where communities come in handy, you know.
Vrijen Attawar: And so we're sort of going back to, as you're discussing today, going back to this idea of, okay, we just need to, like, raise hype.
Vrijen Attawar: We need to bring attention to career span, and we need to essentially, you know, beat the drum until someone says, okay, there's potential here.
Vrijen Attawar: Let's acquire these folks.
Vrijen Attawar: And, you know, one of us, one of Logan and I can stay back, and then Ilsa can stay back, and everyone sort of gets what they want in the end.
Vrijen Attawar: So that would be a nice sort of nice and Tidy way of wrapping things up.
Vrijen Attawar: So that's career span.
Vrijen Attawar: Questions there and thoughts there.
David Speigel: Yeah.
David Speigel: How are you promoting that?
David Speigel: You are well positioned for matching and sourcing because I think you're right.
David Speigel: Like, I wrote a post about this for one of my top clients.
David Speigel: He previously worked at Meta and he wasn't getting any interviews.
David Speigel: And then he was working with me and he switched from application heavy to almost all outreach and then applications.
David Speigel: So he was doing applications and no outreach, and he switched to primarily overloading on outreach and then doing the applications when necessary.
David Speigel: And he told me he's like, one of these places that I applied to, they never heard back from them.
David Speigel: And then I found the hiring manager after I started working with you, and the hiring manager did send me over to the recruiter and both of them said, like, you are the best fit that we've had for this role.
David Speigel: You're a perfect match.
Vrijen Attawar: Right.
David Speigel: Then why didn't you fucking see that for my application?
Vrijen Attawar: Because they're blind.
Vrijen Attawar: They're flying blind.
David Speigel: You can get back to me at all.
Vrijen Attawar: Yeah, no, they're flying completely blind.
Vrijen Attawar: And I, I'm almost like a little concerned about putting that much of a target on my back is how right I know I am that, like, I almost don't want to piss off too many important folks by, like, making a big emperor has no clothes sort of thing out of it.
Vrijen Attawar: Although I am considering it.
Vrijen Attawar: Like I've said, it depends on how you say it.
Vrijen Attawar: Exactly.
David Speigel: I said the same thing a couple different ways.
David Speigel: In some ways are very incendiary and spiky and, you know, rile people up.
Vrijen Attawar: And then you want a little bit of.
Vrijen Attawar: You want to select them out.
David Speigel: Of course, of course.
David Speigel: But this, then the same topic, like, said a little bit more politely and softly, like, still makes the point, right.
Vrijen Attawar: And could theoretically have better cut through.
Vrijen Attawar: So.
Vrijen Attawar: So totally.
Vrijen Attawar: I'm, I'm.
Vrijen Attawar: I'm open to that.
Vrijen Attawar: So, you know, now I'm almost thinking, like, hey, so Ilsa is open to us saying that we can, like, let you scan against the whole Career Span database.
Vrijen Attawar: So I think now I'm almost thinking we recommend.
Vrijen Attawar: Hey, Career Span is for the best of the best.
Vrijen Attawar: We're creating a synthetic data pool, right?
Vrijen Attawar: We have X thousand users that are the best of the best.
Vrijen Attawar: Right.
Vrijen Attawar: You know, you know, with regards to.
Vrijen Attawar: The best of the best.
David Speigel: If you let my.
David Speigel: If you let my Fathom recorder in, then we can use the notes later.
Vrijen Attawar: Oh, nice.
Vrijen Attawar: Yeah, let me let.
Vrijen Attawar: Oh, there's no one at the.
Vrijen Attawar: There's no one knocking on the door.
David Speigel: Oh, mine says recorder and waiting room.
Vrijen Attawar: Huh?
Vrijen Attawar: Oh, Logan's the organizer.
David Speigel: Ah, got it.
David Speigel: Okay.
Vrijen Attawar: Logan's the organizer.
Vrijen Attawar: Let me.
Vrijen Attawar: But mine is running and I can share you on the notes afterwards if you just remind me.
Vrijen Attawar: I have Fireflies lets you also record through the extension.
Vrijen Attawar: So I just generally let that run and then forget about it.
Vrijen Attawar: So that's luckily always running.
Vrijen Attawar: But I say all that to say that.
Vrijen Attawar: Yeah, XK users, best of the best two stories.
Vrijen Attawar: I was just doing an analysis on how effective that message would be.
Vrijen Attawar: But my idea is we can go to these communities and say, hey, essentially we are now scanning across the entire database and all of your folks that put in the effort will get considered for the scan.
Vrijen Attawar: Right.
Vrijen Attawar: And so if you do the two stories, you'll be considered for the scan.
Vrijen Attawar: Or do the three stories, you'll be considered for the scan.
Vrijen Attawar: Employers want something that is like with signal, but the whole thing that has been missing is being able to say to them, hey, just charge us like X amount.
Vrijen Attawar: So what we could say is hey, either pay the flat amount for the thing and take all of the candidates or do the scan for free, but pay us if you accept the candidate into your pipeline.
Vrijen Attawar: Right.
Vrijen Attawar: And we could do one or the other and very easily then leverage that and actually draw folks in by telling them, hey, this is how many employers, this is how many founders scanned career span today.
Vrijen Attawar: So I think there's some really good language here.
Vrijen Attawar: I think there's some really, there's a really good narrative here.
David Speigel: The reason I'm asking that is because I think what you want is like a private vetted community of marquee companies and marquee off market candidates that would consider becoming on market if the right companies were asking over time.
David Speigel: Because people aren't going to just every month if you're a great candidate and somebody pings you every month for a new role, like you can't do that.
David Speigel: And I think that's one things about like when you think of investments you think of liquid and illiquid, right?
Vrijen Attawar: Yeah.
David Speigel: Like someone who's currently working is an illiquid investment, but somebody out of work is a liquid investment for an employer because they're immediately going to be able to transact versus house.
David Speigel: You don't just buy a house and then sell it next month.
David Speigel: Like you have to sit there for a year or two.
David Speigel: Yeah, I feel like, I feel like if, like that's one of the problems about the.
David Speigel: What did you say?
David Speigel: The sourcing is that a lot of employers don't want to source for people who are out of work because they feel like there's a lean towards damaged goods.
Vrijen Attawar: Yeah.
David Speigel: But they can't source for people who are working because there's a lean towards.
David Speigel: Can't move.
Vrijen Attawar: Yeah.
David Speigel: You somehow have to get that channel.
Vrijen Attawar: In the middle of the right time.
Vrijen Attawar: You have to get them at the right time when there's early intense signals that indicate that they're ready to move, but they aren't revealed to everyone.
Vrijen Attawar: Right.
Vrijen Attawar: So that is, it's, it's, it's.
Vrijen Attawar: And it's funny you should say that.
Vrijen Attawar: I've been trying to write a lot of like, put down a lot of like thought, thought leadership, I guess is the douchey term for it, around this idea of like, how do signals transmit in markets, especially signals of human quality and especially signals that are like qualitative and hard to, hard to like parse, you know, effectively.
David Speigel: It would look like this.
David Speigel: Let me see if I got there.
David Speigel: So like you've got people who are working, but they're a side of them that are willing to move.
David Speigel: And you've got people who are working and there's a side of them who are really good, but like just in a spot where they're not working.
David Speigel: And that circle is like the overlap of the population that you want.
David Speigel: People who move and people who are not working but are the highest quality.
David Speigel: They're not the ones that just got discarded.
Vrijen Attawar: I almost, yeah, I almost.
Vrijen Attawar: I mean you could put that on a two by two axis and slap that on the front page of Career span.
Vrijen Attawar: But I do think like being able to say, hey, we'll let you scan against the whole thing.
Vrijen Attawar: And if you pay for your scan, we will actually let you do the incremental scans for free.
Vrijen Attawar: I think that is a good value proposition.
Vrijen Attawar: Right.
Vrijen Attawar: And it's sort of like a bet.
Vrijen Attawar: You're like betting that you will get better people or not.
Vrijen Attawar: And you know, essentially I asked Ilsa, hey, can we have you do it on the increment?
Vrijen Attawar: And she said yes.
Vrijen Attawar: So I actually think that this could be a very powerful, effective.
Vrijen Attawar: And then that gives us a very clear message to the candidates, which is get your two stories in as quickly as possible because people are scanning against career span every single day.
Vrijen Attawar: Right.
Vrijen Attawar: And you don't want to be missed by the scam.
David Speigel: LinkedIn gives you the who viewed my profile?
David Speigel: Kind of a thing.
David Speigel: Yeah, people want to see like, yeah.
Vrijen Attawar: We can even set up a deadline.
Vrijen Attawar: We can say, hey, by noon every day.
Vrijen Attawar: Like noon is when the scan goes out and, you know, we can push people to have that artificial deadline of.
Vrijen Attawar: Okay, this, were, these were the jobs that got surfaced today on Careerspan, right, that you missed out on?
David Speigel: Yeah.
David Speigel: What was the other Scan and then get incremental scans or pay when you hire somebody.
Vrijen Attawar: Yeah, pay when you hire someone.
Vrijen Attawar: So either free scan, but then you pay for the actual candidate you bring into the pipeline and you pay more or you pay less for the scan and you get incremental scans, but you, you don't pay the higher fee for bringing the candidate into your pipeline.
David Speigel: I mean, I kind of feel like maybe you just set up the same.
David Speigel: Like that.
David Speigel: That's LinkedIn, right?
David Speigel: There's.
David Speigel: There's two options for LinkedIn.
David Speigel: There's scanning or there's job posting, right?
Vrijen Attawar: Yeah.
David Speigel: Either you pay to scan your sales navigator or you pay when you hire somebody, which is kind of posting, but you're paying up front for the job posting.
David Speigel: But like you're paying to transact a hire.
David Speigel: You're not scanning.
David Speigel: They're coming to you.
David Speigel: What if you positioned it similar to LinkedIn but like without a lot of the LinkedIn cruft.
Vrijen Attawar: Yeah, I think, I think so.
Vrijen Attawar: Yeah.
Vrijen Attawar: I mean, I think, I think Mark calling it like, I like that douchey ass Sam Altman line of like the densest concentration of talent, like I think we can call it, you know, the densest concentration of talent on any job board.
Vrijen Attawar: Right.
Vrijen Attawar: And say that it is like, you know, a 21st century job board where like all of the work happens in the background.
Vrijen Attawar: You just need to like.
Vrijen Attawar: And it's almost like, yeah, the best candidates can apply, but everyone else can, you know, the best, the best candidates get scanned automatically.
Vrijen Attawar: They get picked up by the scan and everyone else still has a job board they can look at and sort of aspirationally apply to.
Vrijen Attawar: So I actually think that there's.
Vrijen Attawar: Long story short, I think there's a bunch of moving parts there that would work that I think we can like pull off.
Vrijen Attawar: Give me one second.
Vrijen Attawar: I just need to hit the restroom and I'll be right back.
David Speigel: No worries.
David Speigel: It.
David Speigel: What's up?
David Speigel: Okay.
Vrijen Attawar: Yo.
David Speigel: Whoops.
Vrijen Attawar: Can you hear me?
David Speigel: Yes.
Vrijen Attawar: Yeah, so that's that sort of career span in a nutshell.
Vrijen Attawar: It's pretty cool, pretty interesting.
Vrijen Attawar: I think, you know, good potential to be sold in theory.
Vrijen Attawar: We just need to like, iron out a couple of things, you know, more than a couple of things.
David Speigel: But who's your like, ICP buyer target?
Vrijen Attawar: I mean, ideally a big company that can actually pay us something like that can afford to pay us at least, you know, mil.
Vrijen Attawar: 2 mil would be nice.
David Speigel: You know, you want, you want one company like just.
David Speigel: You want a company that is doing hiring as opposed to like a, you want a business that is just looking to hire better as opposed to someone in the career tech space?
Vrijen Attawar: No, we're looking at a company who would benefit from having a, a sourcing play in motion like career span.
Vrijen Attawar: So we can prove that we can just settle into these communities and hoover up a lot of attention and high quality candidates.
Vrijen Attawar: Right.
Vrijen Attawar: And we can demonstrate through repeated scans that like the qu.
Vrijen Attawar: The average number of candidates like being surfaced and picked is increasing.
Vrijen Attawar: Then we can like demonstrate with like a statistical, like with statistical power the likelihood that like we can make more of these matches as we scale the system.
David Speigel: Would that be like a staffing agency?
David Speigel: A recruiting firm?
Vrijen Attawar: Like staffing agency?
Vrijen Attawar: Yeah, exactly.
Vrijen Attawar: Like someone.
Vrijen Attawar: So either company, like I'm about to speak to the founders of Ellie AI, that's a video interview co pilot.
Vrijen Attawar: Like if they're trying to have a sourcing play or if they're trying to go into sourcing or if they'd like to use our tech and integrate that with, as a complement to their video interview as a way of strengthening their products value prop, then that would be one thing that we could do.
Vrijen Attawar: Right.
Vrijen Attawar: And they could acquire us for that.
Vrijen Attawar: I think the team is also largely okay with I think the team is also largely okay with like an Apple hire sort of like situation.
Vrijen Attawar: They just, yeah, they just sort of like need a little clarity and confidence that their paychecks are coming in.
Vrijen Attawar: Right.
Vrijen Attawar: Which.
Vrijen Attawar: Fair enough.
David Speigel: What about like a, a VC talent like group, like a bigger VC firm that has a bunch of companies because then you know that you've got a pool of high quality like let's just pick the top.
David Speigel: Right?
David Speigel: Like a 16Z is like look, we have all these companies, they all want to hire and we need better sources.
Vrijen Attawar: I think the good thing is we can offer.
Vrijen Attawar: We can.
Vrijen Attawar: The good thing with sourcing is the proof is in the pudding, right?
Vrijen Attawar: They just have to try it and they'll see from the candidates surfaced.
Vrijen Attawar: Like what would, what would essentially I think if we're actually able to show, hey, this is the folks that our system surfaced and this is where the deal breaker is on.
Vrijen Attawar: Like if we're able to show pre deal breaker because deal breaker stuff like doesn't live in the right city, needs us visa, blah blah blah.
Vrijen Attawar: Like like I think theoretically what they're asking for is the second list.
Vrijen Attawar: Right?
Vrijen Attawar: But if we show them both lists and say, hey, if you just, like, help us, you know, let's say, like, if you help us promote your job, we can, like, get you more people, you know, or something like that.
Vrijen Attawar: I think we could get more money out of the system.
Vrijen Attawar: So this has actually been a really good.
Vrijen Attawar: It's actually been a wildly good conversation that I may want us to like.
Vrijen Attawar: Actually, would you mind if we did one thing real quick?
Vrijen Attawar: Give me a second.
Vrijen Attawar: Let me.
title: David x Careerspan - Part 1

summary.action_items:
**Vrijen Attawar**
Provide David with notes from Fireflies recording after meeting (11:25)
Follow up with Ellie AI founders for potential partnership/acquisition discussions (21:48)
Continue refining Career Span messaging and value proposition to facilitate acquisition discussions (20:30)

**David Speigel**
Help promote Career Span’s sourcing capabilities by creating and sharing relevant posts and content (09:40)
Assist in framing and polishing language around the value and challenges of hiring matching to appeal to stakeholders (10:20)

**Unassigned**
Explore operationalizing candidate story submission deadlines aligned with daily scans (16:30)
Develop data analytics approach to statistically validate Career Span sourcing efficacy for buyers (21:40)
summary.shorthand_bullet:
🗣️ **Personal Motivation Discussion** (00:01 - 05:28)
Discussed motivation and productivity challenges impacting personal and professional growth
📈 **Career Span Update** (06:30 - 08:00)
Career Span preparing for sale within 2 months, focusing on sourcing over matching due to market skepticism
🔍 **Candidate Pool Definition** (10:30 - 16:30)
Defining a 'best of the best' vetted candidate pool with community-driven submission processes
💰 **Pricing Model Options** (16:00 - 17:40)
Pricing model options: pay-per-scan or pay-per-hire to attract employers, modeled on LinkedIn's business approaches
🤝 **Potential Buyers Identification** (20:20 - 23:59)
Identified possible buyers: staffing agencies, recruiting firms, AI interview tech companies like Ellie AI, VC talent groups
⏳ **Timing Signals Discussion** (13:40 - 15:50)
Discussed timing signals about candidate readiness and challenges of sourcing passive candidates
📊 **Operational Ideas** (16:30 - 21:40)
Operational ideas: daily candidate submission deadlines, statistical proof of source conversion and hire efficacy
summary.overview:
- **Career Span Sale Timing:** Team aims to sell Career Span in two months to meet urgent financial needs and buyer interest.

- **Market Positioning:** Sourcing is paramount; unique candidate pools are key assets, moving away from failed matching technology hype.

- **Monetization Model:** Employers can choose flat fees for scans or pay only upon hiring, enhancing engagement and urgency in candidate selection.

- **Target Acquirers:** Ideal buyers include large companies and staffing agencies seeking robust sourcing tools and proven candidate access.

- **Thought Leadership Strategy:** Balancing honest critiques of hiring market with constructive messaging to position Career Span as a reliable sourcing expert.

- **Operational Efficiency:** Daily scanning schedules will streamline workflows, ensuring timely access to relevant candidates while maintaining sourcing quality.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: I think if you were to reduce all of hiring into a couple of discrete challenges, I think people pretended like they had solved matching, and now that they can't solve matching because the emperor has no clothes, they're switching to verifying and vetting, which is to say is this person is John Smith, actually John Smith from Arkansas, like he claims or whatever.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: And two, as I am evaluating John Smith or Jane Doe or whatever in the process, am I able to evaluate them on an ongoing basis correctly via whether through video interviews or through human interviews, but like AI augmented or, you know, AI augmented interviewers, stuff like that.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: So I think there are a couple of discrete challenges.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: I think we're the only ones that have really solved matching in the sense that we are positioned to solve it if we nail sourcing.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: But what I'm seeing people want to buy is sourcing because they're jaded and they don't really believe that some magic matching tech will save the day.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar

utterances.text: I was just doing an analysis on how effective that message would be.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: But my idea is we can go to these communities and say, hey, essentially we are now scanning across the entire database and all of your folks that put in the effort will get considered for the scan.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Right.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: And so if you do the two stories, you'll be considered for the scan.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Or do the three stories, you'll be considered for the scan.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Employers want something that is like with signal, but the whole thing that has been missing is being able to say to them, hey, just charge us like X amount.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: So what we could say is hey, either pay the flat amount for the thing and take all of the candidates or do the scan for free, but pay us if you accept the candidate into your pipeline.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Right.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: And we could do one or the other and very easily then leverage that and actually draw folks in by telling them, hey, this is how many employers, this is how many founders scanned career span today.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: So I think there's some really good language here.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: I think there's some really, there's a really good narrative here.

utterances.speaker: David Speigel
utterances.text: The reason I'm asking that is because I think what you want is like a private vetted community of marquee companies and marquee off market candidates that would consider becoming on market if the right companies were asking over time.

utterances.speaker: David Speigel
utterances.text: Because people aren't going to just every month if you're a great candidate and somebody pings you every month for a new role, like you can't do that.

utterances.speaker: David Speigel
utterances.text: And I think that's one things about like when you think of investments you think of liquid and illiquid, right?

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Yeah.

utterances.speaker: David Speigel
utterances.text: Like someone who's currently working is an illiquid investment, but somebody out of work is a liquid investment for an employer because they're immediately going to be able to transact versus house.

utterances.speaker: David Speigel
utterances.text: You don't just buy a house and then sell it next month.

utterances.speaker: David Speigel
utterances.text: Like you have to sit there for a year or two.

utterances.speaker: David Speigel
utterances.text: Yeah, I feel like, I feel like if, like that's one of the problems about the.

utterances.text: What did you say?

utterances.speaker: David Speigel
utterances.text: The sourcing is that a lot of employers don't want to source for people who are out of work because they feel like there's a lean towards damaged goods.

utterances.speaker: David Speigel

utterances.speaker: David Speigel
utterances.text: You somehow have to get that channel.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar

utterances.text: So that is, it's, it's, it's.

utterances.text: I've been trying to write a lot of like, put down a lot of like thought, thought leadership, I guess is the douchey term for it, around this idea of like, how do signals transmit in markets, especially signals of human quality and especially signals that are like qualitative and hard to, hard to like parse, you know, effectively.

utterances.speaker: David Speigel
utterances.text: It would look like this.

utterances.speaker: David Speigel
utterances.text: Let me see if I got there.

utterances.speaker: David Speigel
utterances.text: So like you've got people who are working, but they're a side of them that are willing to move.

utterances.speaker: David Speigel
utterances.text: And you've got people who are working and there's a side of them who are really good, but like just in a spot where they're not working.

utterances.speaker: David Speigel
utterances.text: And that circle is like the overlap of the population that you want.

utterances.speaker: David Speigel
utterances.text: People who move and people who are not working but are the highest quality.

utterances.speaker: David Speigel

utterances.text: I mean you could put that on a two by two axis and slap that on the front page of Career span.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: But I do think like being able to say, hey, we'll let you scan against the whole thing.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: And if you pay for your scan, we will actually let you do the incremental scans for free.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: I think that is a good value proposition.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Right.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: And it's sort of like a bet.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: You're like betting that you will get better people or not.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: And you know, essentially I asked Ilsa, hey, can we have you do it on the increment?

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: And she said yes.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: So I actually think that this could be a very powerful, effective.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: And then that gives us a very clear message to the candidates, which is get your two stories in as quickly as possible because people are scanning against career span every single day.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Right.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: And you don't want to be missed by the scam.

utterances.speaker: David Speigel
utterances.text: LinkedIn gives you the who viewed my profile?

utterances.speaker: David Speigel
utterances.text: Kind of a thing.

utterances.speaker: David Speigel
utterances.text: Yeah, people want to see like, yeah.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: We can even set up a deadline.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: We can say, hey, by noon every day.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Like noon is when the scan goes out and, you know, we can push people to have that artificial deadline of.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Okay, this, were, these were the jobs that got surfaced today on Careerspan, right, that you missed out on?

utterances.speaker: David Speigel
utterances.text: Yeah.

utterances.speaker: David Speigel
utterances.text: What was the other Scan and then get incremental scans or pay when you hire somebody.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Yeah, pay when you hire someone.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: So either free scan, but then you pay for the actual candidate you bring into the pipeline and you pay more or you pay less for the scan and you get incremental scans, but you, you don't pay the higher fee for bringing the candidate into your pipeline.

utterances.speaker: David Speigel
utterances.text: I mean, I kind of feel like maybe you just set up the same.

utterances.speaker: David Speigel
utterances.text: Like that.

utterances.speaker: David Speigel
utterances.text: That's LinkedIn, right?

utterances.speaker: David Speigel
utterances.text: There's.

utterances.speaker: David Speigel
utterances.text: There's two options for LinkedIn.

utterances.speaker: David Speigel
utterances.text: There's scanning or there's job posting, right?

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Yeah.

utterances.speaker: David Speigel
utterances.text: Either you pay to scan your sales navigator or you pay when you hire somebody, which is kind of posting, but you're paying up front for the job posting.

utterances.speaker: David Speigel
utterances.text: But like you're paying to transact a hire.

utterances.speaker: David Speigel
utterances.text: You're not scanning.

utterances.speaker: David Speigel
utterances.text: They're coming to you.

utterances.speaker: David Speigel
utterances.text: What if you positioned it similar to LinkedIn but like without a lot of the LinkedIn cruft.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Yeah, I think, I think so.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Yeah.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: I mean, I think, I think Mark calling it like, I like that douchey ass Sam Altman line of like the densest concentration of talent, like I think we can call it, you know, the densest concentration of talent on any job board.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Right.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: And say that it is like, you know, a 21st century job board where like all of the work happens in the background.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: You just need to like.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: And it's almost like, yeah, the best candidates can apply, but everyone else can, you know, the best, the best candidates get scanned automatically.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: They get picked up by the scan and everyone else still has a job board they can look at and sort of aspirationally apply to.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: So I actually think that there's.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Long story short, I think there's a bunch of moving parts there that would work that I think we can like pull off.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Give me one second.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: I just need to hit the restroom and I'll be right back.

utterances.text: No worries.

utterances.speaker: David Speigel
utterances.text: It.

utterances.text: What's up?

utterances.text: Okay.

utterances.text: Yo.

utterances.text: Whoops.

utterances.text: Can you hear me?

utterances.speaker: David Speigel
utterances.text: Yes.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Yeah, so that's that sort of career span in a nutshell.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: It's pretty cool, pretty interesting.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: I think, you know, good potential to be sold in theory.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: We just need to like, iron out a couple of things, you know, more than a couple of things.

utterances.speaker: David Speigel
utterances.text: But who's your like, ICP buyer target?

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: I mean, ideally a big company that can actually pay us something like that can afford to pay us at least, you know, mil.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: 2 mil would be nice.

utterances.speaker: David Speigel
utterances.text: You know, you want, you want one company like just.

utterances.speaker: David Speigel
utterances.text: You want a company that is doing hiring as opposed to like a, you want a business that is just looking to hire better as opposed to someone in the career tech space?

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: No, we're looking at a company who would benefit from having a, a sourcing play in motion like career span.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: So we can prove that we can just settle into these communities and hoover up a lot of attention and high quality candidates.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Right.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: And we can demonstrate through repeated scans that like the qu.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: The average number of candidates like being surfaced and picked is increasing.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Then we can like demonstrate with like a statistical, like with statistical power the likelihood that like we can make more of these matches as we scale the system.

utterances.speaker: David Speigel
utterances.text: Would that be like a staffing agency?

utterances.speaker: David Speigel
utterances.text: A recruiting firm?

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Like staffing agency?

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Yeah, exactly.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Like someone.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: So either company, like I'm about to speak to the founders of Ellie AI, that's a video interview co pilot.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Like if they're trying to have a sourcing play or if they're trying to go into sourcing or if they'd like to use our tech and integrate that with, as a complement to their video interview as a way of strengthening their products value prop, then that would be one thing that we could do.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Right.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: And they could acquire us for that.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: I think the team is also largely okay with I think the team is also largely okay with like an Apple hire sort of like situation.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: They just, yeah, they just sort of like need a little clarity and confidence that their paychecks are coming in.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Right.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Which.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Fair enough.

utterances.speaker: David Speigel
utterances.text: What about like a, a VC talent like group, like a bigger VC firm that has a bunch of companies because then you know that you've got a pool of high quality like let's just pick the top.

utterances.speaker: David Speigel
utterances.text: Right?

utterances.speaker: David Speigel
utterances.text: Like a 16Z is like look, we have all these companies, they all want to hire and we need better sources.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: I think the good thing is we can offer.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: We can.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: The good thing with sourcing is the proof is in the pudding, right?

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: They just have to try it and they'll see from the candidates surfaced.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Like what would, what would essentially I think if we're actually able to show, hey, this is the folks that our system surfaced and this is where the deal breaker is on.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Like if we're able to show pre deal breaker because deal breaker stuff like doesn't live in the right city, needs us visa, blah blah blah.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Like like I think theoretically what they're asking for is the second list.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: Right?

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: But if we show them both lists and say, hey, if you just, like, help us, you know, let's say, like, if you help us promote your job, we can, like, get you more people, you know, or something like that.

utterances.speaker: Vrijen Attawar
utterances.text: I think we could get more money out of the system.

utterances.text: So this has actually been a really good.
